heading: Judicial review of agency board decisions
label: 7107 Judicial review of agency board decisions

children.label: A
children.text: a contractor may appeal the decision to the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit within 120 days from the date the contractor receives a copy of the decision; or

children.label: B
children.text: if an agency head determines that an appeal should be taken, the agency head, with the prior approval of the Attorney General, may transmit the decision to the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit for judicial review under , within 120 days from the date the agency receives a copy of the decision.section 1295 of title 28

children.label: A
children.text: a contractor may appeal the decision to a United States district court pursuant to , within 120 days from the date the contractor receives a copy of the decision; orsection 1337 of title 28

children.label: B
children.text: the Tennessee Valley Authority may appeal the decision to a United States district court pursuant to , within 120 days from the date of the decision.section 1337 of title 28

children.heading: .—Review of arbitration
children.label: 3 .—Review of arbitration
children.text: An award by an arbitrator under this chapter shall be reviewed pursuant to sections 9 to 13 of title 9, except that the court may set aside or limit any award that is found to violate limitations imposed by Federal statute.

children.heading: .—Finality of Agency Board Decisions on Questions of Law and Fact
children.label: b .—Finality of Agency Board Decisions on Questions of Law and Fact

children.text: the decision of the agency board on a question of law is not final or conclusive; but

children.label: B
children.text: so grossly erroneous as to necessarily imply bad faith; or

children.heading: .—Remand
children.label: c .—Remand
children.text: In an appeal by a contractor or the Federal Government from the decision of an agency board pursuant to subsection (a), the court may render an opinion and judgment and remand the case for further action by the agency board or by the executive agency as appropriate, with direction the court considers just and proper.

children.heading: .—Consolidation
children.label: d .—Consolidation
children.text: If 2 or more actions arising from one contract are filed in the United States Court of Federal Claims and one or more agency boards, for the convenience of parties or witnesses or in the interest of justice, the United States Court of Federal Claims may order the consolidation of the actions in that court or transfer any actions to or among the agency boards involved.

children.heading: .—Judgments as to Fewer Than All Claims or Parties
children.label: e .—Judgments as to Fewer Than All Claims or Parties
children.text: In an action filed pursuant to this chapter involving 2 or more claims, counterclaims, cross-claims, or third-party claims, and where a portion of one of the claims can be divided for purposes of decision or judgment, and in any action where multiple parties are involved, the court, whenever appropriate, may enter a judgment as to one or more but fewer than all of the claims or portions of claims or parties.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Whenever an action involving an issue described in paragraph (2) is pending in a district court of the United States, the district court may request an agency board to provide the court with an advisory opinion on the matters of contract interpretation under consideration.

children.heading: .—Applicable issue
children.label: 2 .—Applicable issue
children.text: An issue referred to in paragraph (1) is any issue that could be the proper subject of a final decision of a contracting officer appealable under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Referral to agency board with jurisdiction
children.label: 3 .—Referral to agency board with jurisdiction
children.text: A district court shall direct a request under paragraph (1) to the agency board having jurisdiction under this chapter to adjudicate appeals of contract claims under the contract being interpreted by the court.

children.heading: .—Timely response
children.label: 4 .—Timely response
children.text: After receiving a request for an advisory opinion under paragraph (1), an agency board shall provide the advisory opinion in a timely manner to the district court making the request.
